content: Just as high school English writing instruction frames written reflection as a deliberate practice to make implicit thinking visible to improve long-term writing skill, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to draw on structured, scaffolded reflection routines adapted from second-language writing pedagogy to boost the consistency of autonomous agent reasoning. This cross-pollination turns LLM self-reflection from a niche technical heuristic into a trainable skill, just like writing, that can be improved through repeated guided practice.
